description: This data is derived from sensor stations placed on bridges and surface streets within city limits. Each station has a temperature sensor that measures the temperature of the street surface and a sensor that measures the ambient air temperature at the station each second. Those values are averaged into temperature readings that are recorded by the station every minute. The dataset is updated hourly with new data. Only the most recent 48 hours of data is stored in the dataset.

name: Call Data

columns.name: CAD Event Number
columns.description: The unique number assigned to a CAD Event upon intake.

columns.name: CAD Event Clearance Description
columns.description: This field represents a numerical code that corresponds to a CAD Event disposition, which is logged when the event is cleared by a Call sign. It is also referred to as "disposition desc" in Data Driven. Note: When the clear by code is "G," there are two possible scenarios: If the cc-relation is "S," it indicates a duplicated event. Otherwise, it indicates a canceled event. Additional hardcoded descriptions include: Bad location, Citation, etc.

columns.description: Categorizes units responding to a call. If Unit Type is ‘CT’, it’s a CARE response; otherwise, it’s SPD. If both CARE and SPD respond to the same call, it’s shown as SPD/CARE Co-response. Note that it will show "Error" value when the value of unit type is "Error".

columns.description: A unique alphanumeric code combining a CAD event ID (rin) and the associated dispatched call sign ID (unit id).

columns.name: First CARE Call Sign At Scene Time
columns.description: Timestamp when the first CARE unit arrived at the scene. Null if no CARE unit responded to the call. Note: CARE is a program that addresses non-emergency situations requiring social services or mental health support.

columns.name: First CARE Call Sign Dispatch Time
columns.description: Timestamp when the first CARE unit is dispatched. Null if no CARE unit responded to the call. Note: CARE is a program that addresses non-emergency situations requiring social services or mental health support.

columns.name: First Co-Response Call Sign At Scene Time
columns.description: Timestmap the first unit arrived at the scene. This field is null if only SPD or CARE responded.

columns.name: First Co-Response Call Sign Dispatch Time
columns.description: Timestmap the first unit dispatched. This field is null if only SPD or CARE responded.

columns.name: First SPD Call Sign at Scene Time
columns.description: Timestamp when each SPD unit (all units not “CT”) arrives at the scene. Null if either CARE or SPD responds.

columns.name: First SPD Call Sign Dispatch Time
columns.description: Timestamp when each SPD unit (all units not “CT”) is dispatched. Null if no SPD unit responds or if co-responding with CARE.

columns.name: Last CARE Call Sign In-Service Time
columns.description: Timestamp when last CARE unit completed a call and is ready for the next assignment. Null if no CARE unit responded to the call. Note: CARE is a program that addresses non-emergency situations requiring social services or mental health support.

columns.name: Last Co-Response Call Sign In-Service Time
columns.description: Timestmap the last unit completed a call and is ready for the next assignment. This field is null if only SPD or CARE responded.

columns.name: Last SPD Call Sign In-Service Time
columns.description: Timestamp when each SPD unit (all units not “CT”) completes a call and is ready for the next assignment. Null if no SPD unit responds or if co-responding with CARE

columns.name: CARE Call Sign Total Service Time (s)
columns.description: The total time in seconds for all CARE units from dispatched to when they finish their service times. This measures the overall service duration of CARE units, excluding idle times.

columns.name: Co-Response Call Sign Total Service Time (s)
columns.description: The total time in seconds for all Co-response units from when they dispatched to when they finish their service times. This measures the overall service duration of all units.

columns.name: SPD Call Sign Total Service Time (s)
columns.description: The total time in seconds for all SPD units from dispatched to when they finish their service times. This measures the overall service duration of SPD units, excluding idle times.

columns.name: Call Sign Total Service Time (s)
columns.description: The total time in seconds from Dispatch Time to In-Service Time at the unit id level, indicating the duration a unit spent responding to a call, excluding idle times.

columns.name: First CARE Call Sign Dispatch Delay Time (s)

columns.name: First CARE Call Sign Response Time (s)

description: The Seattle Police Department (SPD) replaced its Records Management System (RMS) in May 2019. To preserve data quality and continuity between systems (2008-Present), SPD relied on the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The standardization of crime classifications allows for comparison over time. For more information on definitions and classifications, please visit https://www.fbi.gov/services/cjis/ucr/nibrs. Additional groupings are used to analyze crime in SPD’s Crime Dashboard.

description: The Fremont Bridge Bicycle Counter began operation in October 2012 and records the number of bikes that cross the bridge using the pedestrian/bicycle pathways. Inductive loops on the east and west pathways count the passing of bicycles regardless of travel direction. The data consists of a date/time field: Date, east pathway count field: Fremont Bridge NB, and west pathway count field: Fremont Bridge SB. The count fields represent the total bicycles detected during the specified one hour period.

columns.name: SiteEUIWN(kBtu/sf)
columns.description: Weather Normalized (WN) Site Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's WN Site Energy divided by its gross floor area (in square feet). WN Site Energy is the Site Energy Use the property would have consumed during 30-year average weather conditions. WN Site EUI is measured in measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: SiteEUI(kBtu/sf)
columns.description: Site Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's Site Energy Use divided by its gross floor area. Site Energy Use is the annual amount of all the energy consumed by the property on-site, as reported on utility bills. Site EUI is measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: SiteEnergyUseWN(kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of energy consumed by the property from all sources of energy, adjusted to what the property would have consumed during 30-year average weather conditions.

columns.name: SourceEUIWN(kBtu/sf)
columns.description: Weather Normalized (WN) Source Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's WN Source Energy divided by its gross floor area. WN Source Energy is the Source Energy Use the property would have consumed during 30-year average weather conditions. WN Source EUI is measured in measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: SourceEUI(kBtu/sf)
columns.description: Source Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's Source Energy Use divided by its gross floor area. Source Energy Use is the annual energy used to operate the property, including losses from generation, transmission, & distribution. Source EUI is measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: GHGEmissionsIntensity

This calculation employs the utility-specific emissions factors defined in the 2023 Building Emissions Performance Standard law.

columns.description: The type of Horizon code. Values include the following: "BorrowerStatClass" = Borrower Statistical Class (e.g. srad, the code for a Seattle Resident Adult). "BorrowerType" = Borrower Type (e.g. br, the code for a regular Borrower). "Grade" = School Grade (e.g. K - 12). "ItemCollection" = Item Collection Code (e.g. nafic, the code for the Neighborhood Library Adult Fiction collection). "ItemStatus" = Item Status (e.g. i for checked in, o for checked out). "ItemType" = Item Type (e.g. acbk, the code for Adult Circulating Books). "Language" = Horizon Language (e.g. SPA for Spanish; ENG for English; null for not given or unknown). "Location" = 3-letter branch/location code (e.g. CEN for Central).

This dataset is useful for understanding the codes used in some of Seattle Public Library's other open datasets. These codes (namely "ItemType" and "ItemCollection") are systematically used in the cataloging of items within Integrated Library System (ILS), Horizon (Sirsidynix).

columns.description: Exact date and time that the survey count ended in ISO 8601 format, yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm

columns.name: using_electronics
columns.description: Engaging with technology, electronics, and digital gadgets in either an introverted (e.g., listening to audio via headphones, conversing on a phone, or reading/writing/playing/working on a computer) or extroverted fashion (e.g., listening to audio via speakers, photographing the surroundings, or interacting with screens in the public realm). Any digital gadget may be included in this category, including but not limited to watches, phones, tablets, and laptops.

columns.name: staying_notes
columns.description: Comments that may serve to clarify the content of the survey data

columns.name: living_public
columns.description: Encamping, lying, or sleeping in an undesignated camping/sleeping location, like on the street or in a square. This type of activity is typically associated with homelessness, and can be recognized by the accompaniment of most personal belongings. Engaged in otherwise private sanitary activities within the public realm. Could be urinating or showering in full or partial visibility of others, in areas that are not designated for these types of activities. This category covers any kind of informal behavior related to sanitary purposes that do not typically take place in public.

columns.name: recreation_active
columns.description: Exercising or playing, either informally or in formally designated areas.

columns.name: staying_mobility_assist
columns.description: Anyone supported by a mobility device. Wheelchairs, canes, walkers, seeing eye dogs, white canes, and if they’re being assisted by another person (do not count infants/children being carried in this category).

columns.name: sitting_formal_private
columns.description: Sitting on furniture intended for seating, but which is privately owned, where the right to sit cannot be purchased by an exchange of goods or money. This can be a chair or a bench in someone’s front garden, furniture that people have brought themselves into public space and which they will take with them upon leaving the public space, or objects intended for sitting on which provide heavy support like a stroller or a wheelchair.

columns.name: sitting_formal_public_movable_seat
columns.description: Sitting down on moveable seating, like bistro chairs, available to the public.

columns.name: sitting_formal_public_fixed_bench
columns.description: Sitting down on a fixed bench available to the public.

columns.name: staying_age
columns.description: The perceived age of people observed.0-4 – Infants, toddlers, (often in strollers, or baby carriers), as well as small children 5-14 –Young children who could be in elementary school, or middle school 15-24 – High school through college age 25-44 – Young adults who look older than college age 45-64 – Middle aged adults, mid/late career, not yet retired 65+ – Retired, older adults

columns.description: Working to upkeep or take care of the public spaces. This could include fixing potholes, sweeping the street, directing traffic, or helping others directions.

columns.name: waiting_transportation
columns.description: Waiting for transportation, whether it is public (e.g., bus, streetcar), private (e.g., car), or commercial (e.g., taxi or rideshare such as Uber, Lyft).

columns.name: talking_to_others
columns.description: Conversing with another person at any tone of voice.

columns.name: taking_care_child

columns.description: People who are perceived by surveyors to be a race or ethnicity. staying_race_ethnicity Key A - Asian B – Black or African American L – Latino/a or Hispanic M – Multiple N – Native American or Alaskan Native P – Pacific Island or Native Hawaiian U – Unsure W – White

columns.name: staying_microclimate
columns.description: Perceived whether condition on the specific survey location

columns.name: staying_gender
columns.description: The perceived gender of people observed (Female, male, unsure).

columns.name: staying_day_of_week
columns.description: Indicates the type of day the data was collected in (weekend or weekday)

columns.name: staying_covid_social_distance
columns.description: If people kept the recommended physical distancing or not. 5 different options; yes, mostly yes, even, mostly no and no

columns.name: staying_covid_mask
columns.description: Whether the person is wearing a face mask/covering or not

columns.name: standing
columns.description: Standing freely in space. They can either be staying still or pacing yet remaining in a small area, unassisted (by wheelchair, etc.), without leaning on anything.

columns.name: soliciting
columns.description: Can include begging for food or money, campaigning, or sex work.

columns.name: smoking
columns.description: Smoking any type of object or substance, whether legal or illegal. Only people visibly smoking should be registered in this category. Some surveys may also categorize people smoking in the activity “Disruptive – intoxicated” if the person smoking is also influenced by the smoked substance to a degree that may cause other people inconvenience or discomfort.

columns.name: sitting_informal
columns.description: Sitting in places not primarily designed for seating, like on the ground, street fixtures, planter, curb, or step. This can include squatting down in space.

columns.name: sitting_formal_public
columns.description: Sitting down on something designed as public seating (benches, picnic tables, etc.).

columns.name: recreation_passive
columns.description: Includes a variety of activities associated with recreational activities typically occurring in place, including: people watching, playing cards, being affectionate with others, reading/writing, creating art (e.g., draining, painting) for personal use or purposes, resting, relaxing, and hanging out.

columns.name: lying
columns.description: Lying down on any surface, awake or asleep

columns.name: leaning
columns.description: Standing while leaning against an object or building, typically in a leisurely way.

columns.name: feeding_child
columns.description: Giving a child/children food

columns.name: disruptive_intoxicated
columns.description: Visibly ingesting alcohol or drugs in an unsanctioned context, depending on the survey location. Showing clear signs of uncontrolled intoxication such as slurred speech, unfocused eyes, aggressiveness, etc.

columns.name: disruptive_aggressive
columns.description: Displaying abusive behavior towards another person or to no one in particular. The behavior can be verbal, physical, or other. The behavior must be assessed as abusive or highly uncomfortable within the context of the survey location.

columns.name: disruptive
columns.description: Displaying abusive behavior towards another person or to no one in particular. Total number.

columns.name: cultural
columns.description: Performing, observing, or participating in cultural activities of artistic, communal, political, or religious character (e.g., outdoor movie, food festival, political rally, music, religious gathering, dance).

columns.name: commercial_selling_informal
columns.description: Selling food or goods in a self-constructed/unpermitted/illegal manner.

columns.name: commercial_selling
columns.description: Selling food or goods in an established/legal setting. Person doing backend activities related to commercial activities, like a waiter busting tables, a person loading commercial goods, or a person setting up a commercial stall.

columns.name: commercial_observing
columns.description: A person who is participating in a commercial situation, without being either a provider or a buyer/shopper in the moment of the survey, is counted as participating by being an observer. This could be a person browsing the produce at a market stall, but who has not yet committed to making a purchase, either by an exchange of money or by queuing up to making a transaction of money.

columns.name: eating_drinking
columns.description: Engaged with consuming food or drinks, either by being in the process of preparing for consumption, being mid-consumption, or post-consumption.

columns.name: commercial_buying
columns.description: In the process of buying foods and goods. Both the person performing a transaction, and the people queuing are counted as buyers.

columns.name: commercial_all

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Calculated based on the known location of a pay station along the same blockface. See Issues section for more info.

columns.name: PaidParkingSubArea
columns.description: A subset of a paid parking area—not all paid parking areas have subareas. Example is Financial, so would be Commercial Core Financial.

columns.name: ParkingTimeLimitCategory
columns.description: In minutes. Options are 120 (2-hour parking), 240 (4-hour parking), 30, or 600 (10-hour parking)

columns.name: BlockfaceName
columns.description: Street segment, name of street with the “from street” and “to street; ” Example is “1ST AVE BETWEEN BELL ST AND BATTERY ST” Combined with Side of Street for Blockface -- 1ST AVE BETWEEN BELL ST AND BATTERY ST SW Side. Also referred to as UnitDesc (description)

columns.name: SourceElementKey
columns.description: Unique identifier for the city street segment where the pay station is located; Use this column to match with SDOT’s Pay Station data and other curbspace asset data

columns.name: SideOfStreet
columns.description: Options are: E, S, N, W, NE, SW, SE, NW. The two digits are because many Seattle streets are at an angle. Avenues run north-south, Streets run east-west. 1 st Ave on the SW side is the west side of the street

columns.name: PaidOccupancy
columns.description: The numerator of the paid occupancy percentage calculation. Number of transactions that have paid at this date/time on this blockface. This is the number of vehicles paid for parking at this time. It is not necessarily the number of vehicles along the blockface since there are most likely vehicles that haven’t paid.

columns.name: ParkingSpaceCount
columns.description: Number of paid spaces on the blockface at the given date and time. This does not include any loading zones (even if payment is required at the loading zones). While SDOT does not delineate individual spaces, we maintain a record of legal parking supply if spaces were marked. Average parallel parking paid space length is 18 feet. Any summary of parking space counts needs to account for the amount of time covered as the data will count all spaces as discrete items over a time period.

columns.name: PaidParkingArea
columns.description: The primary name of a paid parking neighborhood. Example is Commercial Core.

columns.name: OccupancyDateTime
columns.description: The date and time (minute) of the transaction as recorded

columns.name: ParkingCategory
columns.description: An overall description of the type of parking allowed on a blockface. Examples could be Paid or RPZ (restricted parking zone)

columns.name: PaidParkingRate
columns.description: Parking rate charged at date and time. Data is available for the parking rate when SDOT used to have one rate across the entire day, roughly 2012-2015. If the column is blank, that is because the rates were/are currently set by time of day. Rates vary by area or sub area and by morning, afternoon and evening (8-11 am, 11 am-5 or 6 pm, and 5 pm to 8 pm or 10 pm)

description: The City of Seattle has created an on-street paid parking occupancy data set and is providing access to this data set for public use for research and entrepreneurial purposes under the City’s Open Data Program. This data set contains all records for 2019.

columns.name: End of Month

columns.name: Total Storage Days
columns.description: Represents the total number of stroage days for a given month. Storage days are the time a vehicle spends in the impound lot before release.

columns.name: Impounds - Scofflaw
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds performed for a given month classified as scofflaw impounds. A scofflaw impound is a vehicle that has four or more overdue, unpaid parking tickets.

columns.name: Impounds - DWLS
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds performed for a given month classified as DWLS impounds. DWLS stands for driving while license suspended and is determined by the issuing officer.

columns.name: Disposition - Hearing
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds where the vehicle was released by order of a Seattle Municipal Court magistrate.

columns.name: Total Dispositions
columns.description: Represents the total number of impounds where the vehicle was either released to the vehicle owner, was auctioned, or was sold as scrap.

columns.name: Released - Owner Paid
columns.description: Total  number of holds resolved during the month where the vehicle owner paid the associated impound fees.

columns.name: Impounds - Regular
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds performed for a given month classified as regular impounds. Regular impounds are generally defined as impounds related to traffic or incident reports.

columns.name: Total Impounds
columns.description: Indicates total number of impounds that have occurred in a given month. Summation of impounds for regular, scofflaw and DWLS types.

columns.name: Disposition - City Paid
columns.description: Represents the number of regular impounds where the City of Seattle paid the associated impound fees to release the vehicle.

columns.name: Disposition - Auctioned
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds where the vehicle owner did not pay for the vehicle's release and it was auctioned by the tow contractor.

columns.name: Disposition - Sold as Scrap
columns.description: Represents the number of impounds where the vehicle owner did not pay for the vehicle's release and it was sold as scrap by the tow contractor.

columns.name: Storage Days - Regular
columns.description: Represents the total number of storage days for a regular impound for a given month. Storage days are the time a vehicle spends in the impound lot before release.

columns.name: Storage Days - Hold
columns.description: Represents the total number of storage days for an impound in hold status for a given month. Storage days are the time a vehicle spends in the impound lot before release.

columns.name: Storage Days - Scofflaw
columns.description: Represents the total number of storage days for a scofflaw impound for a given month. Storage days are the time a vehicle spends in the impound lot before release.

columns.name: Storage Days - DWLS
columns.description: Represents the total number of storage days for a DWLS impound for a given month. Storage days are the time a vehicle spends in the impound lot before release.

columns.name: Holds Beginning of the Month
columns.description: Total number of impounds in hold status at the beginning of a given month.

columns.name: Holds - Added
columns.description: Total number of impounds in hold status added throughout the month.

columns.name: Released - City Paid
columns.description: Total  number of holds resolved during the month where the City of Seattle paid the associated impound fees.

columns.name: Holds End of the Month
columns.description: Total number of holds remaining at the end of the month.

columns.name: Disposition - DWLS
columns.description: Represents the number of DWLS impounds where the vehicle was released after the tow contractor received payment for the associated impound fees.

columns.name: Disposition - Owner Paid
columns.description: Represents the number of regular impounds where the vehicle owner paid the associated impound fees to release the vehicle.

name: 2020 Paid Parking Occupancy

columns.name: SideOfStreet
columns.description: Options are: E, S, N, W, NE, SW, SE, NW. The two digits are because many Seattle streets are at an angle. Avenues run north-south, Streets run east-west. 1 st Ave on the SW side is the west side of the street

columns.name: ParkingTimeLimitCategory
columns.description: In minutes. Options are 120 (2-hour parking), 240 (4-hour parking), 30, or 600 (10-hour parking)

columns.name: BlockfaceName
columns.description: Street segment, name of street with the “from street” and “to street; ” Example is “1ST AVE BETWEEN BELL ST AND BATTERY ST” Combined with Side of Street for Blockface -- 1ST AVE BETWEEN BELL ST AND BATTERY ST SW Side. Also referred to as UnitDesc (description)

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Calculated based on the known location of a pay station along the same blockface. See Issues section for more info.

columns.name: ParkingCategory
columns.description: An overall description of the type of parking allowed on a blockface. Examples could be Paid or RPZ (restricted parking zone)

columns.name: PaidParkingSubArea
columns.description: A subset of a paid parking area—not all paid parking areas have subareas. Example is Financial, so would be Commercial Core Financial.

columns.name: ParkingSpaceCount
columns.description: Number of paid spaces on the blockface at the given date and time. This does not include any loading zones (even if payment is required at the loading zones). While SDOT does not delineate individual spaces, we maintain a record of legal parking supply if spaces were marked. Average parallel parking paid space length is 18 feet. Any summary of parking space counts needs to account for the amount of time covered as the data will count all spaces as discrete items over a time period.

columns.name: PaidParkingRate
columns.description: Parking rate charged at date and time. Data is available for the parking rate when SDOT used to have one rate across the entire day, roughly 2012-2015. If the column is blank, that is because the rates were/are currently set by time of day. Rates vary by area or sub area and by morning, afternoon and evening (8-11 am, 11 am-5 or 6 pm, and 5 pm to 8 pm or 10 pm)

columns.name: OccupancyDateTime
columns.description: The date and time (minute) of the transaction as recorded

columns.name: PaidOccupancy
columns.description: The numerator of the paid occupancy percentage calculation. Number of transactions that have paid at this date/time on this blockface. This is the number of vehicles paid for parking at this time. It is not necessarily the number of vehicles along the blockface since there are most likely vehicles that haven’t paid.

columns.name: SourceElementKey
columns.description: Unique identifier for the city street segment where the pay station is located; Use this column to match with SDOT’s Pay Station data and other curbspace asset data

columns.name: PaidParkingArea
columns.description: The primary name of a paid parking neighborhood. Example is Commercial Core.

description: The City of Seattle has created an on-street paid parking occupancy data set and is providing access to this data set for public use for research and entrepreneurial purposes under the City’s Open Data Program. This data set contains all records for 2020.

columns.description: Description of the Work from Accela

columns.description: Number of calendar days waiting on the applicant to respond to correction requests or provide additional information.

columns.description: The date SDOT issued the permit. If there is an Application Date but no Issue Date, this generally means the application is still under review.

columns.description: Number of business days waiting on the applicant to respond to correction requests or provide additional information.
